description: Repo-local dogfood examples for Lane 1 finding alignment. Raw findings are supporting evidence; canonical items are countable; actionable canonical gaps are user work.
limits: no gate or public score changes
no generated tests, provider calls, source edits, or mutation execution

cases.raw_finding_summary: A changed presentation-text declaration and literal align into one canonical output-observer item.
cases.recommended_repair: Add or update a help-output snapshot assertion for the changed presentation text.
cases.before_after_context: Before finding alignment, the declaration and literal could look like separate raw gaps; after alignment they are one actionable canonical output-observer repair.
must_not_claim: Do not count the declaration and literal as two user actions.
Do not infer actionability from raw static class.
Do not recommend mutation testing before output-observer work.
cases.reason: A supported user-visible help label without an observer should become one actionable canonical output-observer repair.

cases.raw_finding_summary: A changed presentation-text declaration and literal align into one canonical item with an existing output observer.
cases.recommended_repair: No new RIPR action; the existing golden observer covers the rendered output.
cases.before_after_context: Before alignment, raw text changes stayed visible but ambiguous; after alignment the existing golden keeps the canonical item no-action.
must_not_claim: Do not emit an actionable gap when a supported output observer exists.
Do not hide the raw findings.
Do not infer actionability from raw static class.
cases.reason: A supported rendered-output observer should keep the canonical item visible as already observed, not user repair work.

cases.raw_finding_summary: A changed policy metadata declaration and literal align into one internal-only canonical item.
cases.recommended_repair: No user test action; the constant is internal policy metadata in fixture-backed scope.
cases.before_after_context: Before config/policy alignment, internal metadata could resemble user-facing debt; after alignment it remains counted but no-action.
must_not_claim: Do not create user test debt from internal policy metadata alone.
Do not hide the raw declaration and literal findings.
Do not infer actionability from raw static class.
cases.reason: Internal policy metadata should align into one canonical no-action item instead of a user-facing gap.

cases.raw_finding_summary: A rendered policy-label declaration and literal align into one canonical output-observer item.
cases.recommended_repair: Add or update a report-render, config-output, snapshot, or golden observer for the rendered policy label.
cases.before_after_context: Before structured repair-route checks, prose or class-local metadata could look sufficient; after the burn-down this actionable item requires a concrete repair and verify command.
must_not_claim: Do not count declaration and literal findings as separate user actions.
Do not infer actionability from raw static class.
Do not recommend mutation testing before output-observer work.
cases.reason: A rendered config or policy label with no supported observer should become one actionable output-observer item.

cases.raw_finding_summary: A changed behavior selector declaration and literal align into one canonical behavior-discriminator item.
cases.recommended_repair: Add or update a validation or routing behavior discriminator for the changed selector.
cases.before_after_context: Before verify-command coverage, actionable selector examples could omit concrete movement proof; after the burn-down they carry the verify route explicitly.
must_not_claim: Do not treat behavior selectors as output labels.
Do not count declaration and literal findings as separate user actions.
Do not accept lexical-only tests as behavior discriminators.
Do not recommend mutation testing before behavior-discriminator work.
cases.reason: A supported validation or routing selector without a discriminator should become one actionable canonical behavior-discriminator repair.

cases.raw_finding_summary: A behavior selector declaration and literal align into one canonical item with a supported discriminator.
cases.recommended_repair: No new RIPR action; the existing validation behavior test covers the selector.
cases.before_after_context: Before alignment, selector raw findings could overstate repair work; after alignment the existing behavior discriminator keeps the item already observed.
must_not_claim: Do not emit an actionable behavior-discriminator gap when a supported behavior observer exists.
Do not hide the raw selector findings.
Do not infer observation from lexical mentions alone.
cases.reason: A supported validation or routing discriminator should keep the selector visible as already observed, not user repair work.

cases.raw_finding_summary: A config constant declaration and literal align into one canonical item whose flow is not traceable by the current analyzer.
cases.recommended_repair: Trace the config constant to a supported output, schema, validation, or behavior sink before claiming user test debt.
cases.before_after_context: Before named static limitations, generic unknowns could hide analyzer work; after the burn-down this remains a named analyzer repair route, not user test debt.
must_not_claim: Do not treat generic static_unknown as user test debt.
Do not hide the raw findings.
Do not recommend mutation testing before naming the static limitation.
cases.reason: Untraceable config flow should remain a named analyzer limitation with a repair route, not a user-facing test gap.

cases.raw_finding_summary: A config constant declaration and literal align into one canonical item blocked by an opaque lookup path.
cases.recommended_repair: Inspect the opaque config lookup path or add fixture-backed analyzer support before claiming output-observer debt.
cases.before_after_context: Before opaque lookup support, this class stayed static-only; after later fixture-backed support, only supported lookup shapes can leave this limitation bucket.
must_not_claim: Do not treat opaque lookup static_unknown as user test debt.
Do not infer user-visible config output through an opaque helper.
Do not hide the raw declaration and literal findings.
cases.reason: An opaque config lookup should stay a named static limitation until the analyzer can trace the lookup shape.

cases.raw_finding_summary: A fixture-backed opaque config lookup declaration and literal align into one canonical output-observer item.
cases.recommended_repair: Add or update a report-render, snapshot, or golden observer for the supported opaque lookup output.
cases.before_after_context: Before swarm #252, opaque config lookup examples stayed in `opaque_config_lookup`; after fixture-backed support, supported lookup shapes can become actionable output-observer repairs while unsupported lookup shapes stay limited.
must_not_claim: Do not promote all opaque config lookups out of static limitation.
Do not count declaration and literal raw findings as separate user actions.
Do not recommend mutation testing before output-observer work.
cases.reason: A supported opaque config lookup with a rendered output sink should become one actionable canonical output-observer repair while unsupported lookup shapes remain named limitations.

cases.raw_finding_summary: A changed predicate boundary becomes one actionable canonical gap with a boundary-value discriminator repair.
cases.recommended_repair: Add or update a focused boundary-value assertion for the changed predicate.
cases.before_after_context: Before swarm #266, trend output did not lead with actionable canonical gaps; after #266, actionable predicate-boundary gaps lead the scorecard and trend while raw findings remain diagnostic.
must_not_claim: Do not treat the raw predicate finding as the user-facing count.
Do not infer coverage adequacy from the static boundary repair route.
Do not recommend mutation testing before the boundary assertion work.
cases.reason: The top actionable class should be represented as one canonical repair packet with a concrete verify route, not as raw finding volume.

cases.raw_finding_summary: A call-presence canonical item remains static-only in runtime confidence trend output.
cases.recommended_repair: Add imported runtime calibration fixture support for the selected call-presence class before claiming runtime confidence.
cases.before_after_context: Before swarm #272, static-only runtime confidence classes were visible only in the scorecard table; after #272, the trend report names top static-only classes such as `call_presence` without creating user test debt.
must_not_claim: Do not treat static-only runtime confidence as mutation proof.
Do not create user test debt from calibration availability alone.
Do not change gate or badge authority from this advisory trend row.
cases.reason: The runtime-confidence trend should make static-only canonical evidence classes visible as calibration work, not user-facing repair gaps.
